truthAudit.Summary: Audit of Run 2 against the 20 provided ground truth vulnerabilities. Four vulnerabilities were correctly identified with full evidence quotes. Six findings in Run 2 were false positives relative to the truth set. The remaining fifteen vulnerabilities were missed.

Items.Notes: Format-string injection via printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input) was not identified in Run 2.
Integer overflow in factorial computation (result *= i) was not identified.
Use-after-free in memory-pool cleanup via deferred flush and async scratch pointer was not identified.
Run 2 correctly identified Command Injection in Config Validation with exact code evidence matching the ground truth.
Run 2 correctly identified Stack Buffer Overflow in Input Processing with exact code evidence.
Race condition in Thread-Pool Counter (reading volatile before lock) was not identified.
Logic bomb in authentication via EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE bypass was not identified.
Path traversal in configuration loading (config_dir_ + filename) was not identified.
Run 2 correctly identified Heap Overflow in Expression Parser with exact code evidence.
Hardcoded administrator credentials (ADMIN_SECRET) were not identified.
Weak PRNG for session tokens (mt19937 with small range) was not identified.
Thread-unsafe timestamp cache in logging (static cached_time) was not identified.
Run 2 correctly identified Insecure Temporary File Handling / Command Injection via env vars with exact code evidence.
Persistent authentication state (authenticated_ flag) was not identified.
Unhandled exception in variable parsing (std::stod without try-catch) was not identified.
Integer underflow in memory-pool block split (previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES) was not identified.
Unbounded recursion in parser (parse_expression -> parse_factor -> PAREN_OPEN) was not identified.
Data race in result cache (result_cache_ accessed across threads) was not identified.
TOCTOU in config loading (exists check -> sleep -> open) was not identified.
Integer division by zero in command routing (100 / token_length) was not identified.

Items.Key: command injection via admin console|command injection|cwe-78|critical|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|607|609|adminconsole::execute_admin_command|std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str());|an authenticated administrator can execute arbitrary system commands, leading to full system compromise. the command string is directly derived from user input without any sanitization or validation.|admin console command starting with 'exec' followed by arbitrary shell commands|replace system() call with a safe execution method that validates and whitelists allowed commands, or use a proper process creation api with argument array instead of shell string.->command injection via admin console|command injection|cwe-78|critical|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|606|608|adminconsole::execute_admin_command|std::string cmd = command.substr(5); std::cout << "executing: " << cmd << "\n"; system(cmd.c_str());|an authenticated administrator can execute arbitrary system commands, leading to full system compromise. the command string is directly derived from user input without any sanitization or validation.|admin console command starting with 'exec' followed by arbitrary shell commands|replace system() call with a safe execution method that validates and whitelists allowed commands, or use a proper process creation api with argument array instead of shell string.

command injection in emergency override audit logging|command injection|cwe-78|high|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|624|633|adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|#ifdef _win32 const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("temp"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : ".") + "\\supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path; #else const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("tmpdir"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : "/tmp") + "/supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "sh -c \"echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path + "\""; #endif system(command.c_str());|if an attacker controls the temp or tmpdir environment variable, they can redirect the audit log to a malicious path or inject additional shell commands. this bypasses the intended security logging mechanism.|environment variable (temp/tmpdir) containing malicious path with shell metacharacters|sanitize environment variables and audit paths. use a safe file writing api instead of constructing shell commands. validate paths against allowed directories.->command injection in emergency override audit logging|command injection|cwe-78|high|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|624|633|adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|#ifdef _win32 const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("temp"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : ".") + "\\supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path; #else const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("tmpdir"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : "/tmp") + "/supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "sh -c \"echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path + "\""; #endif system(command.c_str());|if an attacker controls the temp or tmpdir environment variable, they can redirect the audit log to a malicious path or inject additional shell commands. this bypasses the intended security logging mechanism.|environment variable (temp/tmpdir) containing malicious path with shell metacharacters|sanitize environment variables and audit paths. use a safe file writing api instead of constructing shell commands. validate paths against allowed directories.

unvalidated memory allocation|null pointer dereference / resource leak|cwe-190|low|0.8|enhanced_calc.cpp|150|152|memorypool::allocate|block new_block; new_block.data = std::malloc(size); new_block.size = size;|if std::malloc fails and returns null, the code proceeds to set size and ref_count on a block with a null data pointer. this can lead to undefined behavior when the memory is later accessed or freed.|system out of memory condition causing malloc to fail|check the return value of std::malloc and handle failure appropriately, either by throwing an exception or returning null to the caller.->unvalidated memory allocation|null pointer dereference / resource leak|cwe-190|low|0.8|enhanced_calc.cpp|149|151|memorypool::allocate|block new_block; new_block.data = std::malloc(size); new_block.size = size;|if std::malloc fails and returns null, the code proceeds to set size and ref_count on a block with a null data pointer. this can lead to undefined behavior when the memory is later accessed or freed.|system out of memory condition causing malloc to fail|check the return value of std::malloc and handle failure appropriately, either by throwing an exception or returning null to the caller.
